title: Chouchou Refuses to Abandon the Shop
description: On pages 5–8, Chouchou’s loyalty to the pet food shop turns Buggy’s occupation from a pirate threat into a personal wound for the town.
scene: Chouchou refuses to give up the place connected to his owner, while pirates and bystanders react to the dog’s stubborn defense.

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: You don't know when to quit! Is your favorite food in there, doggy?

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: CHOUCHOU! HOW MANY TIMES HAVE I TOLD YOUNOT TO EAT THE MERCHANDISE!

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: awright, chouchou. You're in charge of the shop while I'm in the hospital.

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Boodle
dialogue.text: Heh heh! That's the smartest, bravest dog in town, Miss!

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: I'm going to look for Zolo.

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: I'd better find him before that weird costume guy does.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Don't talk crazy! Next time, that lion will eat you alive!

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: WHAT KIND OF FOOL WOULD PIT A DOG AGAINST ME?

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Boodle
dialogue.text: Well, ME'AL NEVER SELL FET FOOD in this town again...

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: And I think that chouchou still guards the store...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: ...because it's all that's left of his beloved master.

summary: This part makes the conflict emotional: Luffy sees that Buggy’s violence is not abstract, but aimed at things people love and protect.
